en: Why did the lighthouse refuse to date the buoy? It was tired of all the ups and downs.
Why do lighthouses make great therapists? They've seen every kind of storm and still shine.
The lighthouse said to the ship, "You're looking a little wrecked." The ship replied, "You should see the rocks."
Lighthouses never panic. They just keep turning in circles until things improve.
Why are lighthouses bad at keeping secrets? They always spill the light.
Lighthouses don't judge the storms — they just keep shining anyway.
Why did the lighthouse keeper never get lonely? He always had a bright outlook on life.
What do you call a lighthouse with great people skills? A real beacon of hospitality.
Why don't lighthouses ever tell secrets? They can't help shining a light on everything.
What did the harbor say to the returning boat? "Long time, no sea!"
Why did the seagull bring a napkin to the picnic? Just in case things got a little fishy.
What do you call a perfectly calm morning at the harbor? A pier-fect start to the day.
Why did the lighthouse get promoted? It always had a shining record.
What's a sailor's favorite day of the week? Tide-sday.
What do you call a lighthouse that tells great jokes? A real beacon of comedy.
Why did the old dock feel appreciated? Everyone kept saying, "You're the best, pier none."
Why did the fog apologize to the harbor? It didn't mean to make things unclear.
Why do lighthouses make terrible liars? Their light always gives them away.
What do you call a grandmother who loves the sea? A tide-and-true soul.
Why did the crab never want to share his snacks? He was a little shellfish.
What did the lighthouse say on its hundredth birthday? "Still shining after all these years."
What do you call two lighthouses that are best friends? Double the beacon, double the hope.
Why did the harbor throw a party every evening? To welcome all the boats home.
Why did the buoy get invited to every party? It always knew how to stay afloat.
What did the compass say to the lighthouse? "Thanks for always pointing me in the right direction."
Why was the little boat so confident? Its captain always told it, "You've got this, no matter the waves."
What do you call a wave that tells the best stories? A real tide-teller.
Why did the old fisherman stay so calm? He knew every storm passes, and the harbor's always waiting.
What do you call a lighthouse on a cloudy night? Still the most important light in town.
Why did the sunrise get a standing ovation at the harbor? It never once missed a show.
Why did the calm trader bring tea to his desk? Because patience pairs well with the market.
What did the steady investor say to the nervous one? "Slow and steady still gets you to harbor."
Why do the best traders love lighthouses? They both know the value of staying the course through the fog.
